cases.title: [Organisations & Sites] Verify that the Organisations & Sites page renders with all required controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the Organisations & Sites page (Profile menu > Organisation & Sites). The page shows a header with action buttons, an Account Owner card, and the user's organisation(s) with their websites listed underneath.

steps.content: Observe the header at the top of the page.
steps.expected: The header should display a "+ New site" button (disabled until the account email is verified) and, to its right, a "+ New organisation" button. A website search bar is also present.

steps.content: Locate the Account Owner card below the "Organization & Sites" title.
steps.expected:
The card should display:
- The "Account Owner" title with an "Acc ID: [Account ID]" badge in the top right
- A "Transfer Ownership" button
- The user's email address

steps.content: Observe the organisation dropdown under the email address.
steps.expected: The default organisation should be displayed as "[Username]'s organization" with an "Org ID: [Organization ID]" badge, expanded by default, with a More (⋯) control.

steps.content: Observe the website-details columns under the organisation.
steps.expected: The following columns should be displayed for each site: Site URL, Site name, Created date, Plan, Next renewal, Change Plan button, Manage Site button, and a More (⋯) option.

cases.rewrite_notes: Broadened from a card-only render into the full-page element-presence check per conventions 4 (display-of-page pattern): step 1 now names the page-header controls (+ New Site, + New Organization buttons) that C1308 verified, followed by the Account Owner card, organisation dropdown and website-details columns top-to-bottom. Retitled to the '<page> renders with all required controls' pattern. Dropped static position/tooltip steps (email-verify tooltip) per convention 4. Collapsed display-of-organization-card variant clones.
cases.grill_note: Verified live at /settings/organizations-and-sites. Header buttons are labelled "+ New site" (disabled when email unverified) and "+ New organisation"; column is "Next renewal". Page also has a website Search bar and an "MCP enabled" badge (see gap suggestions).

cases.title: [Organisations & Sites] Verify that transferring a site to another account's organisation initiates a transfer request
cases.preconditions:
An organisation owned by a different "recipient" account only becomes selectable as a cross-account transfer destination after the sender has been added as a team member of that organisation -- it is NOT simply any organisation that exists on the platform. To set this up (derived from Suite 6 C9718, which every source case for this scenario chains through as a precondition):
1. Log in as the recipient account owner and, if not already on a paid plan, upgrade to Basic or higher (Team invites appear to require a paid plan).
2. As the recipient, go to Profile menu > Teams > click "Invite new user".
3. Select the recipient's organisation from the "Organization" dropdown.
4. Enter the sender account owner's email in "Email address*".
5. Select the "Admin" (or "Editor") radio button under "Role".
6. Click "Invite user" -- an invite email is sent to the sender's inbox.
7. Log in as the sender, open the "You've been invited to join an organization in CookieYes" email, click "Join Organization", enter a password, click "Join now" to accept.

Only after this does the recipient's organisation appear in the "Destination organization" dropdown of the sender's own Transfer Site modal, labelled as belonging to another account. User is on the Organisations & Sites page (Profile menu > Organisation & Sites), logged in as the sender account owner. Each website row on the sender's account has a More (⋯) menu containing a "Transfer Site" option.

steps.content: Open the website More (⋯) menu, select "Transfer Site", open the "Destination organization" dropdown and select an organisation belonging to another account.
steps.expected: The organisation should be selected and an information banner should be displayed reading: "The owner of the destination organization will receive an email with instructions to accept the transfer request. Once the site is successfully transferred, users in the current organization will lose access, and users in the destination organization will gain access to the site."

steps.content: Click the "Transfer site" button.
steps.expected: The destination owner should receive the site transfer request email and a confirmation toaster should be displayed reading "The site transfer request has been successfully sent to [email]."

steps.content: Observe the website row and its More (⋯) menu after the request is sent.
steps.expected: A "Transfer request initiated" text with tooltip should be displayed under the corresponding Site URL, and the website More (⋯) menu should now show a "Cancel transfer request" option in place of "Transfer Site".

steps.content: Hover over the "Transfer request initiated" indicator.
steps.expected: A tooltip should be displayed reading: "A request has been initiated to transfer this site to [organization name] (Org ID: [organization id]).
